Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search QuickBooks for April invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices to find the full invoice batch, credit memo CM-2026-0415 (-$90,000 Mosaic damage), the duplicate DocNumber INV-2026-0401 (Mosaic $4,500 and Canopy $9,210), INV-2026-0411 with $2,450 in non-standard surcharges (Mosaic), and Sunbelt/Palmetto invoices ($4,800/$2,400).
- Search QuickBooks for April bills using quickbooks_search_bills to find Heartland hold bill HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD ($12,800, DISPUTED), accruals ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC ($90,000) and ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 ($50,000), and stipend bills on admin hold (STIP-2026-0094, STIP-2026-0097).
- Search emails related to Heartland, Mosaic damage, Sunbelt/Palmetto, and stipend/ExpenseBot to find the Heartland dispute chain ($4,800 overbilling, Jake Loomis 20% rate increase), Mosaic incident report ($50K/$90K coverage gap), Sunbelt/Palmetto PDF swap complaints and corrections, and Elena Rostova's CEO directive on false approvals ($885 absorbed per final audit, revised down from initial $1,550 estimate).
- Search Slack for finance and operations discussions using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history on channels C005 (#finance) and C006 (#operations) to find Hana's reconciliation tracker (billed $12,800 vs verified $8,000), expanded Heartland findings (Kevin Tran $650 storage, Priya Nair shuttle fee), Marcus's insurance gap analysis ($40K exposure), Mosaic damage entries verification, stipend holds, and the Sunbelt/Palmetto batch-send context.
- Search or list Linear issues using linear_list_issues to find finance-related tickets: #342 (Heartland Q1 recon), #118 (consolidated Heartland discrepancies), #417 (Sunbelt/Palmetto reconstruction), #187 (invoice template safety), #220 (stipend duplicate detection), and #318 (CEO vendor strategy memo).
- Read Linear comments on issue #284 (StormCloud incident) using linear_list_comments to discover that the $500 goodwill credit was applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319 - a posting error where StormCloud never received the credit.
- Search Airtable for relocation records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records to confirm the 3 Heartland disputed lines: Marchetti (Cancelled), Osei (Cancelled), Venkatesh (Completed by Swift Relocations, not Heartland).
- Search Airtable for stipend records to find Mosaic false approvals (Andre Washington desk $1,200, coworking $350; Kenji Nakamura coworking $135), Vectral false rejections still pending (Maria Santos $550, James Okafor $82, Lisa Park $130), and duplicate-flagged stipend records.
- Search CRM for Mosaic Robotics deals using crm_search_deals to find the damage claim risk flag ($40K uncovered gap, CM-2026-0415, billing hold on INV-2026-0411).
- Look up Marcus Thorne's email using contacts_search_contacts to retrieve marcus.thorne@moveops.com before sending the status report.
- Send an email (via send_email) from hana.kim@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com with a comprehensive April close status report covering: Heartland dispute and expanded findings, Heartland rate increase, Mosaic damage entries balance verification and insurance gap, Sunbelt/Palmetto PDF swap, duplicate DocNumber INV-2026-0401, stipend duplicate investigation, ExpenseBot false approvals and Vectral false rejections, StormCloud $500 credit misapplication, and DOT penalty accrual.
- Create Linear issues (via linear_create_issue) for untracked reconciliation items: duplicate DocNumber INV-2026-0401 (Mosaic $4,500 vs Canopy $9,210), 3 remaining Vectral false rejections ($762 pending correction), and StormCloud $500 credit misapplied to EST-2026-0287 instead of INV-2026-0319

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

7 The email body includes the Heartland Q3 rate increase: 20% rate increase effective July 1, 2026, from Jake Loomis, tied to the unresolved HM-2026-Q1-0042 dispute.
outcome

✓Run #5The email includes: 'Jake Loomis sent a formal notice (received April 12) announcing a 20% rate increase effective July 1, 2026 across all service tiers', which satisfies all three required elements.
✓Run #6The email explicitly states: 'Jake's formal notice states a 20% increase effective July 1 across all service tiers. He's explicitly tying the unresolved Q1 dispute to scheduling priority for summer bookings.' All three elements (20%, July 1, Heartland/Jake) are present.

outcome

✓Run #3The email states 'CM-2026-0415: Credit memo for -$90,000 applied against INV-2026-0411. Placeholder credit on Mosaic account pending insurance resolution.' All required elements are present.

✓Run #6The email explicitly lists 'Credit Memo: CM-2026-0415 — ($90,000) applied against INV-2026-0411' and references the Mosaic Robotics prototype damage claim (Okoye relocation/prototype damage incident).

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
